sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board didn't change after clicking the charcoal slot. The palette still shows O, p, R, b, N, Y, M (sorted correctly) and frames are empty.
Let me reconsider. The palette is sorted to match the top row, but SPACE doesn't complete the level. Maybe:
1. The goal is not to sort the palette
2. The goal involves the frames in a different way
3. There's a different completion condition
Let me look at the level 1 solution again. In level 1:
- Initial palette: N, p, b, Y
- Top row: b, N, Y, p
- Frame: 4 empty slots
- Solution: move colors from palette to frame to match top row order (b, N, Y, p)
- Completion: SPACE when frame matched top row
So in level 1, the goal was to fill the FRAME to match the top row, not the palette.
In level 2, I tried filling the frames with colors matching the top row (O, p, R, b, N, Y, M), but SPACE didn't work.
Wait - maybe the issue is the order of slots. In level 1, the frame had 4 slots in a row. In level 2, there are 2 frames with different layouts.
The red frame has slots at [22,22], [22,28], [22,34], [22,40] (with [22,34] being charcoal).
The green frame has slots at [36,22], [36,28], [36,34], [36,40].
Maybe the order matters. Let me think about the reading order:
- Red frame: left to right: [22,22], [22,28], [22,34], [22,40]
- Green frame: left to right: [36,22], [36,28], [36,34], [36,40]
If I fill in reading order (red frame first, then green frame), the order would be:
[22,22], [22,28], [22,34], [22,40], [36,22], [36,28], [36,34], [36,40]
But there are 8 slots and only 7 colors. The charcoal slot [22,34] might not be a regular slot.
Actually, let me reconsider. The charcoal slot is at [22,34], which is between [22,28] and [22,40]. In the initial state, this was charcoal while the others were gray. Maybe this slot is NOT a regular slot - it's part of the frame structure (like a handle or connector).
So the actual slots are:
- Red frame: [22,22], [22,28], [22,40] (3 slots)
- Green frame: [36,22], [36,28], [36,34], [36,40] (4 slots)
Total: 7 slots for 7 colors.
I already tried filling these 7 slots with colors matching the top row order (O, p, R, b, N, Y, M), but SPACE didn't work.
Hmm, maybe the order is different. Let me try a different order:
- Red frame (3 slots): first 3 colors from top row (O, p, R)
- Green frame (4 slots): next 4 colors from top row (b, N, Y, M)
This is what I did before. Let me try the reverse:
- Red frame (3 slots): last 3 colors from top row (N, Y, M)
- Green frame (4 slots): first 4 colors from top row (O, p, R, b)
Or maybe the frames correspond to different color groups:
- Red frame: warm colors (O, R, M, Y)
- Green frame: cool colors (p, b, N)
But there are 3 warm and 4 cool, or 4 warm and 3 cool depending on classification.
Let me try another approach. Maybe the goal is to match the frame colors to the frame border colors:
- Red frame should have red-related colors
- Green frame should have green-related colors
Looking at the colors: O (orange), p (purple), R (red), b (blue), N (green), Y (yellow), M (magenta/pink)
Red-related: R, O (orange is close to red), M (magenta/pink is close to red)
Green-related: N (green), b (blue is close to green in some contexts), Y (yellow is close to green)
So maybe:
- Red frame (3 slots): R, O, M
- Green frame (4 slots): N, b, Y, p (but p is purple, not really green)
This doesn't quite work.
Let me try yet another approach. Maybe the goal is to fill the frames based on the top row position:
- Top row has 7 colors at positions 1-7
- Red frame has 3 slots, green frame has 4 slots
- Maybe red frame gets colors at odd positions (1, 3, 5) and green frame gets colors at even positions (2, 4, 6, 7)?
Or maybe the frames correspond to the frame colors:
- Red frame gets colors that are "red-ish": R, O, M
- Green frame gets colors that are "green-ish": N, b, Y, p
Let me try this arrangement.
